why: activateDefaultTyping(..., DefaultTyping.NON_FINAL, As.PROPERTY) is the Redis value mapper; NON_FINAL emits no type info for the final Result wrapper, so read-back as Object.class throws the Jackson 'cannot construct instance / missing type information' error. Typing strategy (or property style) must be changed so the wrapper round-trips.

The standard API response wrapper that gets cached. Declared 'public final class' with only a private constructor and no @JsonTypeInfo/@JsonCreator, so DefaultTyping.NON_FINAL skips it and Jackson cannot reconstruct it from the cached value. Must be made deserializable (drop final / add type info / add a Jackson creator) so it round-trips.

Shared JsonMapper builder (LocalDateTime/Instant serializers + date format) reused by JacksonCodec; the time-field formatting that comes back inconsistent after a cache round-trip is governed here, so the time (de)serializer registration must be applied consistently to the cache mapper.
